title: Draw + test wayfinding inventory for one facility type (~40 symbols)
context: open-pictograms serves two jobs done well: communication and wayfinding. For M1 to reach ≥100 shipped symbols, a full wayfinding inventory for one facility type (e.g. clinic, shelter, or transit hub) must be drawn and tested alongside the core communication vocabulary. Wayfinding symbols are directional and facility-specific, requiring attention to RTL/mirror variants, universal facility iconography, and local cultural conventions for direction and navigation.
objective: Draw, lint, comprehension-test, culturally-review, and export approximately 40 wayfinding symbols covering a complete inventory for one target facility type; each symbol reaching ≥67% correct comprehension with the target population.
acceptanceCriteria: Approximately 40 symbols covering a complete wayfinding inventory for one defined facility type (clinic, shelter, transit hub, or school); the facility type is documented in the task output and is consistent with the partner channel's needs (or with M0's candidate partner list if no partner is yet confirmed).

Symbols cover: facility zones (entrance, exit, reception, waiting, consultation, toilet, emergency), services (water, food, pharmacy, first aid, information, registration), directions (left, right, straight, up, down), and transport/access modes relevant to the facility.
All symbols are original vector geometry with originality attestation; RTL/mirror directional variants produced for directional symbols; all pass the automated style-lint gate and CI build.
Each shipped symbol has a recorded comprehensionScore at ≥67% correct comprehension with the target population; below-threshold symbols are redesigned and retested or withheld.
Cultural review completed for all people/place/gesture symbols; metadata and provenance records complete; no license or originality violations.
resources: open-pictograms-design-001 (design system v1 — incl. directional variant rules)
output: symbols/ (~40 original SVGs including directional variants), metadata/ (~40 JSON records with comprehension scores), testing/ (aggregated de-identified comprehension results), and CI-generated exports (PNG/sprite/OBF); all CC-BY-4.0. PR opened against main.
